title: Empowering Law Enforcement To Fight Sex Trafficking Demand Act of 2025
short_title: Empowering Law Enforcement To Fight Sex Trafficking Demand Act of 2025
stage_label: Referred to the House Committee on the Judiciary.
plain_summary: This bill changes the rules for how police departments can spend federal crime-fighting money called Byrne JAG grants. Before this bill, police could not use this money for certain programs to stop human trafficking. Now police departments will be allowed to use these grants to pay for programs that fight human trafficking and try to reduce the demand for trafficked people. This gives law enforcement more tools and funding options to combat sex trafficking in their communities.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on the Judiciary.

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Empowering Law Enforcement To Fight Sex Trafficking Demand Act of two thousand twenty five.

sections.title: Section 2 Additional Authorized Use of Byrne JAG Funds
sections.text: This section changes an existing law called the Omnibus Crime Control and Safe Streets Act of nineteen sixty eight. It adds a new way that police departments and other law enforcement agencies can spend money from Byrne JAG grants. These grants help local police fight crime. Now police can also use this grant money for programs that fight human trafficking. This includes programs that try to stop people from buying or using trafficked persons. Human trafficking means forcing people to work or sell their bodies against their will.
